nodes.label: Tactus — Deaf-accessible guitar-coaching wearable (README)
nodes.rationale: Top-level project overview; fuses webcam + mic through a learned physical model to recover what was played and give one precise correction felt on the body and seen on the hand.

nodes.label: LEARN mode (Rocksmith for Deaf players)
nodes.rationale: One of two modes on one engine: feel the target song on your body, play it on a real guitar, and on any mistake the system rewinds, replays correct haptics vs yours, and shows the physical fix with Claude vision highlighting the wrong finger.

nodes.label: PLAY mode (real-time tuning by feel)
nodes.rationale: Pick up the guitar and get real-time flat / sharp / in tune / on the beat feedback by feel.

nodes.label: The one correction (3 axes: note, duration, pressure)
nodes.rationale: Three axes — wrong note, wrong duration, pressure (too-light vs good; too-hard surfaces as a sharp/choked pitch fault). Each is a measured signal cross-checked across vision + audio; no AI-invented score.

nodes.label: Locked software split (browser vision + Python audio over localhost WebSocket)
nodes.rationale: Browser owns camera + MediaPipe + ArUco + AR + viz; Python owns mic + F0 + fusion model + 12-ch haptic output; they sync over one localhost WebSocket with vision features flowing browser->Python, timestamped.

nodes.label: 12-channel haptic body map (6 back strings + 6 torso fret-zones)
nodes.rationale: 12 channels = 6 STRING sites on the BACK (back_high_e..back_low_e) + 6 FRET-ZONE sites on the TORSO (torso_z1..torso_z6); all 12 torso-mounted amps, strings on back, fret-zones on front/torso. Bound by 6 SK473 amps x 2 ch = 12. Live source of truth is config/channel_map.json.
